title: Flaky Fun on National Croissant Day
story: Buttery, flaky croissants get their own special day on January 30! People celebrate National Croissant Day by visiting bakeries, trying new flavors, and sharing photos of their favorite croissants online. Some folks make croissants at home, though it takes hours of rolling and folding dough. Restaurants offer special croissant sandwiches and desserts. The crescent-shaped pastry originally came from France, but now everyone can enjoy this delicious treat that's perfect for breakfast or anytime snacking.
activity_question: What makes croissants special to bake at home?
activity_choices: They take hours of rolling and folding dough
They only need five minutes to make
activity_answer: They take hours of rolling and folding dough
bonus_fact: A croissant has over 80 layers of buttery dough folded together!

title: Silly Messages for Your Machine Day
story: People go completely bonkers with their voicemail greetings on January 30! Inane Answering Message Day encourages everyone to ditch boring "leave a message" recordings for totally ridiculous ones instead. Families create funny voices, sing silly songs, or pretend to be robots answering their phones. Some people record their pets barking the message or make up crazy stories about why they can't answer right now!
activity_question: What do people change on Inane Answering Message Day?
follow_up_question: What would be the silliest voicemail message you could record?

title: Dino-mite Drawing Day!
story: Grab your crayons and get ready to roar! National Draw a Dinosaur Day happens every January 30, when kids and grown-ups everywhere pick up pencils to sketch their favorite prehistoric creatures. Some people draw fierce T-Rex with giant teeth, while others create colorful long-necked Brontosaurus or spiky Triceratops. Art teachers love this day because students get super excited about drawing. Many families make it a fun activity by drawing dinosaurs together, then hanging their masterpieces on the refrigerator. Social media fills up with dinosaur doodles as people share their creations online. Whether you're an amazing artist or just love to scribble, this day celebrates creativity and our fascination with these incredible ancient animals that lived millions of years ago.
activity_question: What do people commonly do on National Draw a Dinosaur Day?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite dinosaur and what colors would you use to draw it?

title: Pop Goes the Bubble Wrap Day!
story: Pop, pop, POP! Every January 30, people around the world celebrate Bubble Wrap Appreciation Day by doing what we all secretly love – popping those satisfying little air bubbles! Offices hold bubble wrap popping contests to see who can pop the most bubbles in one minute. Kids bring sheets of bubble wrap to school for stress-relief activities. Some people even create bubble wrap art or use it for dance parties, stomping on giant sheets. Social media fills with videos of people popping bubble wrap in creative ways. It's the one day when making noise with packaging material is totally encouraged!
activity_question: What do offices sometimes hold on Bubble Wrap Appreciation Day?
follow_up_question: What other everyday items do you think deserve their own appreciation day?

title: National Escape Day: Break Free Fun!
story: Every January 30, National Escape Day gives everyone permission to break free from boring routines! People celebrate by doing something totally different from their usual day. Some escape to new places like museums, parks, or even just a different room in their house. Others escape into books, video games, or creative projects. Families might have backwards dinner for breakfast or build epic blanket forts. The best part? There are no rules about how to escape - you just have to do something that feels like an adventure!
activity_question: What do people do on National Escape Day?
follow_up_question: What would be your perfect way to escape from your normal routine?

title: Pack Your Dreams Day!
story: January 30 is National Plan for Vacation Day, when families everywhere pull out maps, browse websites, and dream about their next big adventure! People spend the day researching fun destinations, comparing prices, and marking calendars for future trips.
activity_question: What do families typically do on National Plan for Vacation Day?
activity_choices: Research destinations and plan future trips
Actually go on vacation that day
Pack their suitcases for no reason
Only plan trips to the grocery store
activity_answer: Research destinations and plan future trips
follow_up_question: Where would you most want to plan a vacation to visit?

title: Backwards Day: Everything's Topsy-Turvy!
story: On January 30, people celebrate National Backwards Day by doing everything in reverse! Kids wear their clothes backwards, walk backwards to school, and say "goodbye" when they arrive. Some families eat dessert first, then dinner. Teachers might start lessons at the end and work toward the beginning. People write their names backwards and try talking in reverse sentences. Social media fills with upside-down photos and backwards videos. The day started as a fun way to shake up boring routines and see the world differently. Even some stores join in by displaying signs backwards or letting customers shop in reverse order through their aisles!
activity_question: What do people commonly do with their clothes on National Backwards Day?
follow_up_question: What would be the most challenging thing for you to do backwards for a whole day?

title: Seeds of Fun: National Swap Day
story: Gardeners across the country gather on January 30 for National Seed Swap Day, trading packets of flower, vegetable, and herb seeds like they're swapping baseball cards! Libraries, community centers, and garden clubs host special events where people bring seeds they've saved from last year's plants and exchange them for new varieties. Kids love discovering unusual seeds like purple carrots or giant sunflowers that can grow taller than a house. Many swappers share growing tips and stories about their favorite plants, making new friends while planning their dream gardens for the upcoming season.
activity_question: Where do people commonly gather to swap seeds on this holiday?
follow_up_question: What unusual plant would you most want to grow in your garden?

title: Art Makes Hearts Happy Day
story: Every January 30, people celebrate National Inspire Your Heart with Art Day by filling their lives with creativity! Families visit museums, draw pictures together, or make crafts at home. Some people share their artwork on social media or try a new art form they've never done before. Schools might have special art projects, and libraries often host painting or sculpting activities. The goal is simple: let art make your heart feel good and maybe discover a hidden talent you didn't know you had!
activity_question: What do families often do together on National Inspire Your Heart with Art Day?
activity_choices: Only watch TV shows about art
follow_up_question: What kind of art would you try for the first time on this day?
